parsed_review.summary: CP00-611 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (40 RP20 units, RP20.P01.M02.S09-RP20.P01.M04.S06, risk B) continuing the RP20 "Data Room And VDR" P01 model foundation from CP00-610 (which ended at RP20.P01.M02.S08) and handing off to CP00-612 (RP20.P01.M04.S07). It is the current live-open pack: docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json sets actual_first_planned_pack_id/generated_pack_start_pack_id=CP00-611 (risk B, unit_count 40, matching range, deviation_from_plan=false, source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65... matching the review baseline), so the baseline correctly trusts the live queue per the standing directive. The pack honestly claims production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status in_progress, implementation_layer=descriptor in both manifest.json and command-evidence.json — no over-claim of production readiness or enterprise trust, consistent with never weakening production_ready gates. The validators' production_ready_candidate=true is a clearly-scoped candidate flag, not a final production_ready assertion. Coverage reconciles across review-baseline included_units, registry.js CP611_SECTION_ROWS (MODEL_TAIL_ROWS=12, MODEL_FULL_ROWS=22, P01_MODEL_ROWS.slice(0,6)=6), validators.js expected counts, the 6 model.test.js tests, manifest delivery_counts/micro_phase_row_counts, and the contract projection: 40 covered_units, 3 sections (M02=12, M03=22, M04=6), deliverable distribution implementation 23 / ui 5 / fixture 2 / test 6 / hermes_evidence 2 / claude_review 2. Title-based deliverable typing in service.js (dataRoomVdrCoreDeliverableType) matches the baseline per-unit deliverable_type (State transition map/Required field registry/Package directory layout->ui, Fixture model->fixture, Model/Invalid reference/Ownership drift tests->test, Hermes model summary->hermes_evidence, Claude model review prompt->claude_review, Documentation entry/Index export check->implementation). Tests draw the plan pack from the live closeout-pack-plan.json (manifest snapshot only as fallback) and assert coverage, runtime-closed boundaries, and full contract deep-equality of projections AND the embedded cp611 descriptor; scripts/validate-rp20-data-room-vdr-core-contract.mjs (wired as rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate) deep-equals contract program/projections/no_write_attestation/descriptor against code, so any divergence fails closeout. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: freezeCp611Result forces every runtime/dispatch/write/storage/audit/Hermes-receipt flag false and synthetic_only/no_real_data/omits_unauthorized_data true, backed by CP611_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (incl. model/validation/fixture/test_runtime_opened=false) and the nine required_no_leak_guards; source contains no fs writes or product runtime (validators/service return frozen objects; the validate script and tests only read for verification). Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer (allowed_claude_tools Read/Grep/Glob; descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, asserted by the validator). Hermes patterns are confined to the deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; receipt_shape descriptor_only_data_room_vdr_p01_model_foundation_continuation_no_write; program runtime_opened=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ review hardening guards are enumerated in forbidden_review_evidence (not_logged_in, zero_byte_output, malformed_json, fenced_json_only, tool_call_shaped_output, missing_raw_output, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, enterprise_trust_claim); readiness-report auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1445-byte valid JSON stdout) with no preserved invalid attempts and prompt 2240 bytes (no full diff/source). The UI progress-control-room edits, workbook/* matter docs, the v2.0 docx, and docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json are correctly listed as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved with active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[], so no in-scope implementation file was dropped and no unrelated dirty file was pulled in. Two non-blocking P3 findings: (1) command-evidence.json records commands:[] with hermes_gate.status "pending" and gate_outcome "pending_review" — the expected pre-closeout/pre-review state (matching how CP00-610 was reviewed), not a readiness over-claim, but the listed validation commands and Hermes gate H20 must be executed/captured at exit 0 before production_ready flips true; (2) the CP00-611 descriptor validator omits the dispatches_rfi_runtime/cp_runtime/closing_binder_runtime/access_analytics_runtime assertions that CP00-610's equivalent validator includes — a minor defense-in-depth asymmetry whose effective boundary still holds because those flags are frozen false in freezeCp611Result and deep-equal-verified against the contract. No P0/P1/P2 findings; nothing in this review blocks pack or goal closeout. Final human approval and any enterprise-trust closure remain outside this read-only review receipt.

findings.title: Pre-review command-evidence is unpopulated (commands [], Hermes gate pending) at review time
findings.details: command-evidence.json records "commands": [] with hermes_gate.status "pending" (command_ids rp00_control_plane_contract_validate, spec_requirement_validate), gate_outcome "pending_review", status in_progress, and production_ready false. Unlike the closed CP00-610, whose post-review command-evidence captured node --check/--test, rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate, npm test, npm run validate/build, weighted/spec/fullplan/goal/rp00/rp05 validate, closeout-pack(-plan):validate, and git diff --check all at exit 0, CP00-611 has no captured local/Hermes validation evidence yet. This is consistent with the honest pre-closeout state (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor) and is NOT a production-readiness or enterprise-trust over-claim, so it does not block this read-only review. The source (registry/service/validators/tests/contract/validator) is internally consistent and the validation gate is enforced by the post-review closeout automation, not by this review.
findings.recommendation: During the post-review closeout sequence, execute and capture all listed validation commands (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, rp05:matter-core:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check, plus rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate) at exit 0 and flip Hermes gate H20 / gate_outcome to passed before setting production_ready true. Do not close the pack on the empty command-evidence.

findings.title: CP00-611 descriptor validator omits four dispatch-flag assertions present in the CP00-610 validator
findings.details: validateDataRoomVdrCoreCp611P01ModelFoundationContinuationDescriptor (lines 187-213) asserts dispatches_data_room_runtime and dispatches_vdr_runtime are false but, unlike validateDataRoomVdrCoreCp610ScopeContractFoundationDescriptor (lines 76-81), does not assert dispatches_rfi_runtime, dispatches_cp_runtime, dispatches_closing_binder_runtime, or dispatches_access_analytics_runtime are false. This is a defense-in-depth asymmetry only: freezeCp611Result hardcodes all four flags to false on the frozen descriptor, and the contract deep-equality test (model.test.js / rp20:data-room-vdr-core:validate) compares the full descriptor against contracts/data-room-vdr-core-contract.json, which records all four as false (lines 2825-2828) — so any drift to true would fail closeout. There is no functional gap, but the dedicated descriptor gate is marginally weaker than the prior pack's, which is worth noting given the directive never to weaken readiness gates.
findings.recommendation: For parity with the CP00-610 validator, add assertCondition checks that descriptor.dispatches_rfi_runtime, dispatches_cp_runtime, dispatches_closing_binder_runtime, and dispatches_access_analytics_runtime are false in validateDataRoomVdrCoreCp611P01ModelFoundationContinuationDescriptor. Non-blocking; the effective boundary is preserved by the frozen values and the contract deep-equality check.
